target.name: Etched
target.founder: Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, Robert Wachen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Etched is a Series A chip design company valued at $5.0B post-money following $125M+ in total fundraising. Valuation assessment draws from a peer set of eight private chip companies—including d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI at Series A—alongside three public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) for sector context.

Two methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the $5.0B last post-money valuation, implying $10.0B at next stage. Peer-Median triangulation across three same-stage, same-sector peers yields $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $2.0B; this reflects significant dispersion within the Series A chip cohort. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Etched's revenue is not disclosed, precluding P/S multiple application despite a 24.4x median P/S across public semiconductor benchmarks.

The triangulated range is $850M (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median of $5.5B across two methods. The current $5.0B valuation sits near the median, suggesting fair-market pricing relative to peer stage progression and recent private comps. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and growth trajectory—both material to public-market multiples—and the persistence of valuation dispersion within the Series A chip cohort, which spans $500M to $2.0B.
